vllm.author: Potabk
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Considering that we cannot provide effective error information description in the original logic (logic that cannot be entered), we need raise the exception in advance

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Replace print with logger

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Calculate correct block size (in bytes) for CPU MLA

vllm.title: [Build/CI] Update lm-eval to 0.4.8
vllm.author: cthi
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This updates the lm-eval test dep to 0.4.8, so we could use new features such as the vllm-vlms backend.

vllm.title: [Kernel] Add more dtype support for GGUF dequantization
vllm.author: LukasBluebaum
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This adds bfloat16 and float32 support for the GGUF dequantization, so no additional cast is necessary anymore.

vllm.title: [core] Add tags parameter to wake_up()
vllm.author: erictang000
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Addresses #15254
Adds optional tags parameter for all calls to wake_up (for both online and offline mode). Previous behavior for calling wake_up() with no parameters should remain unchanged (will reallocate both weights and kv_cache together), but now the user has the option to call wake_up(tags=["w

vllm.title: [V1] Fix json_object support with xgrammar
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Our previous behavior with xgrammar was to ensure that the response was
valid JSON. When the json_object output format is requested, the correct
behavior is to ensure the response is a JSON object. Valid JSON could
also be an array, a string, or a number, which is not desired here.
The fix is to exp

vllm.title: Add minimum version for `huggingface_hub` to enable Xet downloads
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Using Xet to to download models from HF will increase download speeds.
You can read more about Xet on the Hub here.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Benchmarks] Ensure `async_request_deepspeed_mii` uses the OpenAI choices key
vllm.author: b8zhong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Deepseed-MII benchmarking fails with
KeyError: 'text'
Fails because the deepspeed-mii endpoint for returns generated text under choices[0]["text"] instead of a top-level "text" key. Updating the parser to extract from the "choices" array resolves this.
Added backward compat. just in case

Bug reprod

vllm.title: [CI] Remove duplicate entrypoints-test
vllm.author: yankay
vllm.bodyExcerpt: After merging #14868, we noticed there are two entrypoint tests running in the same pipeline. This duplication is unnecessary, so this PR removes the redundant entrypoint test from our CI workflow.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix the issue where the model name is empty string, causing no response with the model name.
vllm.author: chaunceyjiang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix the issue where the model name is empty string, causing no response with the model name.
PR #13568 introduced a new feature that allows vLLM to respond normally even when model_name is None.
i.e.
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct

curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \
     -

vllm.title: [Metrics] Hide deprecated metrics
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Hide the following metrics that were deprecated in 0.8:

vllm:num_requests_swapped
vllm:cpu_cache_usage_perc
vllm:cpu_prefix_cache_hit_rate
vllm:gpu_prefix_cache_hit_rate
vllm:time_in_queue_requests
vllm:model_forward_time_milliseconds
vllm:model_execute_time_milliseconds

As per https://docs.vllm.a

vllm.title: [Frontend] Implement Tool Calling with `tool_choice='required'`
vllm.author: meffmadd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR will implement tool_choice='required' for the Tool Calling API, a requested feature for the OpenAI-compatible API server (#10526 and #13002).
FIX #10526
FIX #13002
Roadmap
1. Validation and Guided Decoding

 Extend validation to support tool_choice='required'.
 Implement structured decoding

vllm.title: [CPU][Bugfix] Using custom allreduce for CPU backend
vllm.author: bigPYJ1151
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Current IPEX allreduce has conflict with vLLM CPU thread binding. This PR:

Replaced IPEX allreduce with custom implementation
Updated related doc

vllm.title: Restricted cmake to be less than version 4 as 4.x breaks the build of…
vllm.author: npanpaliya
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
… pytorch/pyarrow
Fixes the build failure on IBM/Powerpc64le. The build failure was seen while building pytorch and pyarrow with newer cmake. Pinning cmake<4 fixes the issue. Same issue is reported in pytorch as well pytorch/pytorch#150149

vllm.title: [misc] instruct pytorch to use nvml-based cuda check
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We are suffering a lot from torch.cuda.is_available(), which unintentionally initializes cuda runtime. It has two disadvantages:

it makes the process not fork-able. and we need to use export VLLM_WORKER_MULTIPROC_METHOD=spawn
it makes the cuda runtime read CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES and decide the gpus t

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
VLLM_USE_V1=1 python examples/offline_inference/vision_language.py -m mistral3
...
The image depicts a picturesque scene featuring the iconic Tokyo Skytree, a tall broadcasting and observation tower located in Tokyo, Japan. The tower is framed by a canopy of cherry blossom (sakura) trees in full blo

vllm.title: Fix `huggingface-cli[hf-xet]` -> `huggingface-cli[hf_xet]`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Should resolve issues with TPU and ROCm installs.
Alternative to #15963

vllm.title: [V1][TPU] TPU-optimized top-p implementation (avoids scattering).
vllm.author: hyeygit
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Top-k and top-p are slow on TPU because existing algorithms use torch.scatter. For some reason torch.scatter is extremely slow on TPU. There's ongoing work to optimize it, but until that's done, we need an alternative algorithm that circumvents scattering.
The algorithm in this PR avoids torch.scatt

vllm.title: [TPU] optimize the all-reduce performance
vllm.author: yaochengji
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Before the PR, the all-reduce performance is not optimal due to two reasons:

on v6e-8, XLA compiler accidentally apply 2D-ring strategy, while 1D-ring is expected
The ring-order cannot be automatically adjusted

vllm.title: [V1][TPU] Do not compile sampling more than needed
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We're compiling a few extra graphs for sampling that we should never come across at runtime.
Namely the number of requests that require sampling can never exceed the number of tokens that are generated.
Eg. when we get N=16 tokens in input, we are not going to get 128 sequences for sampling.  So onl

vllm.title: [Benchmark] Add AIMO Dataset to Benchmark
vllm.author: StevenShi-23
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR addresses issues #15378. It adds AIMO datasets which contain 3 huggingface datasets for reasoning tasks: AI-MO/aimo-validation-aime, AI-MO/NuminaMath-1.5, and AI-MO/NuminaMath-CoT.
Also, it facilitates reproducibility by passing random seed to hf dataset constructor, because shuffling takes
